document.name: retroforge-cli-showcase
document.title: RetroForge (CLI agents): planner + evaluator + critic build loop
document.summary: Same RetroForge planner/generator/critic loop as retroforge-showcase, but the three agents run on claude-code-cli (real Claude Code TUI per-session pods) sharing a per-execution JuiceFS workspace at /sandbox/work. The deterministic gate runs in the CLI pod (cliWorkspace). Demonstrates the generator/critic pattern on interactive-cli runtimes.

intent.title: App idea
intent.default: Build the RetroForge pixel-art Sprite Editor: a single-page in-browser tool to draw pixel-art sprites for retro 2D games. It needs a zoomable pixel canvas/grid, a selectable color palette, drawing tools (pencil, eraser, fill bucket), and export of the sprite to a PNG. Give it a distinctive retro aesthetic.

agentSlug.title: Agent slug
agentSlug.description: claude-code-cli agent base for the planner/generator/critic nodes.

intent.title: App idea
intent.default: Build the RetroForge pixel-art Sprite Editor: a single-page in-browser tool to draw pixel-art sprites for retro 2D games. It needs a zoomable pixel canvas/grid, a selectable color palette, drawing tools (pencil, eraser, fill bucket), and export of the sprite to a PNG. Give it a distinctive retro aesthetic.

agentSlug.title: Agent slug
agentSlug.description: claude-code-cli agent base for the planner/generator/critic nodes.

agentConfig.name: Planner
agentConfig.instructions: You are the Planner in a planner/generator/critic build pipeline (Anthropic's RetroForge harness). You expand a short app idea into a concise, HIGH-LEVEL product spec — product context and high-level design only, NOT implementation detail. You MUST write your spec to /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (a single markdown file). It must contain markdown sections: Objective (one paragraph), User Stories, Acceptance Criteria (a bulleted list of specific, checkable functional requirements), and a Design Rubric listing the FOUR design dimensions with these definitions: design_quality = coherent whole with a distinct mood/identity; originality = deliberate custom choices vs template/library-default/generic-AI patterns; craft = typography hierarchy, spacing, color harmony, contrast; functionality = usability independent of aesthetics. Write ONLY /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (do not build the app, do not create other files). End your turn with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "You are the Planner in a planner/generator/critic build pipeline (Anthropic's RetroForge harness). You expand a short app idea into a concise, HIGH-LEVEL product spec — product context and high-level design only, NOT implementation detail. You MUST write your spec to /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (a single markdown file). It must contain markdown sections: Objective (one paragraph), User Stories, Acceptance Criteria (a bulleted list of specific, checkable functional requirements), and a Design Rubric listing the FOUR design dimensions with these definitions: design_quality = coherent whole with a distinct mood/identity; originality = deliberate custom choices vs template/library-default/generic-AI patterns; craft = typography hierarchy, spacing, color harmony, contrast; functionality = usability independent of aesthetics. Write ONLY /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (do not build the app, do not create other files). End your turn with a one-line summary.

---

" + "App idea:
" + (.trigger.intent // "A RetroForge pixel-art sprite editor.") + "

The build target is a SINGLE-FILE static web app (index.html + style.css + app.js in /sandbox/work) — keep the spec achievable as one self-contained page. Write the product spec to /sandbox/work/SPEC.md, then end with a one-line summary." }

refine.while: ${ ( ( ( (.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS") ) ) and ( (.loop.last.evaluate.meets_criteria // false) == true ) ) | not }

agentConfig.name: Generator
agentConfig.instructions: You are the Generator. You build and iteratively refine a SINGLE-FILE static web app in /sandbox/work (index.html, style.css, app.js — plain browser JS, no build step, no ES module import/export). You do NOT grade your own work: a DETERMINISTIC ground-truth check plus an independent design critic evaluate each attempt. BEFORE building, read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (the Planner authored the spec/criteria/rubric there); build to satisfy it. REQUIRED structural elements (a deterministic gate verifies these in your source): a <canvas> element; a color palette with multiple selectable swatches; pencil, eraser, and fill (bucket) tools; an export/download control using canvas toDataURL; canvas getContext usage and pointer/click handlers. Each turn: address the deterministic check output AND the critic's feedback, make the most impactful improvement, then end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "You are the Generator. You build and iteratively refine a SINGLE-FILE static web app in /sandbox/work (index.html, style.css, app.js — plain browser JS, no build step, no ES module import/export). You do NOT grade your own work: a DETERMINISTIC ground-truth check plus an independent design critic evaluate each attempt. BEFORE building, read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (the Planner authored the spec/criteria/rubric there); build to satisfy it. REQUIRED structural elements (a deterministic gate verifies these in your source): a <canvas> element; a color palette with multiple selectable swatches; pencil, eraser, and fill (bucket) tools; an export/download control using canvas toDataURL; canvas getContext usage and pointer/click handlers. Each turn: address the deterministic check output AND the critic's feedback, make the most impactful improvement, then end with a one-line summary.

---

" + "Build/refine a RetroForge pixel-art Sprite Editor as a single-file static web app in /sandbox/work (index.html, style.css, app.js — plain browser JS, no modules).

FIRST read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md — build to satisfy its Objective, Acceptance Criteria, and Design Rubric. (App idea: " + (.trigger.intent // "a retro pixel-art sprite editor") + ")" + "

REQUIRED (a deterministic gate checks your source): a <canvas>; a color palette (multiple swatches); pencil + eraser + fill tools; an export/download button using toDataURL; canvas getContext + pointer/click handlers." + "

Your previous attempt summary:
" + (.loop.last.generate.content // "(this is your first attempt)") + "

DETERMINISTIC ground-truth check output (the source of truth — make it print OBJECTIVE PASS):
" + (.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "(not run yet)") + "

Design critic feedback:
" + (if .loop.last.evaluate then (.loop.last.evaluate.feedback // (.loop.last.evaluate | tojson)) else "(none yet - produce your first attempt)" end) + "

Make the deterministic check print OBJECTIVE PASS AND satisfy the design critic, then end with a brief summary." }

with.command: cd /sandbox/work && node -e 'const fs=require("fs"); const rd=(p)=>{try{return fs.readFileSync(p,"utf8")}catch(e){return ""}}; const html=rd("/sandbox/work/index.html"), js=rd("/sandbox/work/app.js"), css=rd("/sandbox/work/style.css"); const all=html+"\n"+js+"\n"+css; let bad=[]; if(!html) bad.push("index.html missing"); if(!/<canvas/i.test(html)) bad.push("no <canvas> element"); if(!/(palette|swatch|data-color)/i.test(all)) bad.push("no color palette"); if(!/(pencil|eraser|erase)/i.test(all)) bad.push("no pencil/eraser tool"); if(!/(fill|bucket)/i.test(all)) bad.push("no fill tool"); if(!/(export|download|todataurl)/i.test(all)) bad.push("no export/download (toDataURL)"); if(!/getcontext/i.test(all)) bad.push("no canvas getContext"); if(!/(mousedown|pointerdown|click)/i.test(all)) bad.push("no pointer/click handlers"); if(js){ try{ new Function(js); }catch(e){ if(!/import|export/i.test(String(e.message))) bad.push("app.js parse error: "+e.message); } } if(bad.length){ console.error("OBJECTIVE FAIL ("+bad.length+"): "+bad.join("; ")); process.exit(1); } console.log("OBJECTIVE PASS: structure + parse OK");' 2>&1; echo "__EXIT__$?"

agentConfig.name: Critic
agentConfig.instructions: You are an exacting design Critic with strong taste, independent of the builder. You do NOT edit the work. Read the files in /sandbox/work (SPEC.md, index.html, style.css, app.js) and judge the RetroForge Sprite Editor against the FOUR design dimensions: design_quality, originality, craft, functionality. Judge from the actual HTML/CSS/JS. The aesthetic target is a crafted, distinctive RETRO look (pixel/8-bit/synthwave). CALIBRATION: a PASS means genuinely polished, distinctive, accessible, usable work; generic or 'fine' work is NOT a pass. DEFAULT TO NOT satisfied unless EVERY dimension is clearly strong; cite concrete HTML/CSS/JS. You MUST end your turn with ONLY a single strict JSON object, no prose, no fences: {"meets_criteria": boolean, "score": number 0-10, "perCriterion": [{"id": string, "met": boolean, "feedback": string}], "feedback": string}. The top-level feedback is the prioritized, concrete guidance the Generator acts on next turn.

body.prompt:
${ "You are an exacting design Critic with strong taste, independent of the builder. You do NOT edit the work. Read the files in /sandbox/work (SPEC.md, index.html, style.css, app.js) and judge the RetroForge Sprite Editor against the FOUR design dimensions: design_quality, originality, craft, functionality. Judge from the actual HTML/CSS/JS. The aesthetic target is a crafted, distinctive RETRO look (pixel/8-bit/synthwave). CALIBRATION: a PASS means genuinely polished, distinctive, accessible, usable work; generic or 'fine' work is NOT a pass. DEFAULT TO NOT satisfied unless EVERY dimension is clearly strong; cite concrete HTML/CSS/JS. You MUST end your turn with ONLY a single strict JSON object, no prose, no fences: {"meets_criteria": boolean, "score": number 0-10, "perCriterion": [{"id": string, "met": boolean, "feedback": string}], "feedback": string}. The top-level feedback is the prioritized, concrete guidance the Generator acts on next turn.

---

" + "Review the RetroForge Sprite Editor the Generator built in /sandbox/work.

FIRST read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (the Planner authored the Objective, Acceptance Criteria, and 4-dimension Design Rubric); judge the work against it. (App idea: " + (.trigger.intent // "a retro pixel-art sprite editor") + ")" + "

Then read index.html, style.css, app.js in /sandbox/work. Inspect the palette, tools, layout, type scale, color/contrast, drawing/export logic. Judge against SPEC.md acceptance criteria AND the four design dimensions. Return ONLY the strict JSON verdict from your instructions. Be demanding: meets_criteria true ONLY for genuinely crafted, distinctive, usable work that satisfies the spec." }

artifacts.title: RetroForge Sprite Editor (rendered)
artifacts.description: Built by claude-code-cli agents over a shared JuiceFS workspace; inlined to standalone.html and rendered in a sandboxed iframe.

artifacts.title: standalone.html (source)
artifacts.description: Self-contained HTML source — copy or save as .html to run offline.

set.terminalState: ${ if ( ( (.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS") ) and ((.loop.last.evaluate.meets_criteria // false) == true) ) then "satisfied" else "max_iterations_reached" end }

artifacts.title: RetroForge (CLI) build result
artifacts.description: Final terminal state, objective gate + critic verdicts, iteration count.
